clusters.headline: Hegseth announces in Brussels a review of U.S. forces in Europe, and a 'NATO 3.0'
clusters.summary: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth lashed out at NATO allies on Thursday, announcing a review of American forces in Europe, and calling for a reboot of the organization to turn it into a "NATO 3.0."

clusters.headline: Talks on implementing US-Iran deal still planned in Switzerland on Friday: Bern
Search for six-year-old Ebola patient after armed men storm DR Congo hospital
clusters.summary: Health facilities have come under attack during the current outbreak as a result of misinformation and fear.

clusters.headline: Lawyer in high-profile Ugandan treason case charged with related offence
clusters.summary: Uganda's military chief had boasted of "the hurt and pain" he would inflict on Erias Lukwago, who was arrested earlier this week.

clusters.headline: Gunfire and explosions heard at Niger capital's airport
clusters.summary: Niger has been fighting a militant Islamist insurgency for a decade and in January suspected jihadists attacked the same airport.

clusters.headline: South Africa: Xenophobia in South Africa - State's Complicity With Gangs and Vigilantes Is Threatening Its Ability to Govern
clusters.summary: [The Conversation Africa] Marches, Mozambicans murdered, state-sponsored evacuations, a nationally televised presidential address. Anti-immigrant mobilisation has again drawn the world's attention to South Africa. The continental backlash threatens tourism, trade, diplomacy and investment opportunit

clusters.headline: South Africa: Old Video Falsely Linked to Xenophobic Attacks in South Africa Was Filmed Months Earlier in Another Province
clusters.summary: [Africa Check] Old video falsely linked to xenophobic attacks in South Africa was filmed months earlier in another province

clusters.headline: Sudan: MSF Sacks 18 Staff in Eastern Chad for Sexual Exploitation of Sudanese Women and Underage Girls
clusters.summary: [Dabanga] El Tina, Eastern Chad / Amsterdam -- The medical NGO Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF) has confirmed that 18 members of its staff in eastern Chad have been dismissed for serious misconduct, "and are now barred from working with MSF", after "serious allegations of sexual exploitation

clusters.headline: Africa: Money, Food and Survival - What Drives Paid Sex Among Young Mums in 3 African Countries
clusters.summary: [The Conversation Africa] Transactional sex, defined as the exchange of sex for money, food, or favours, is common among young people in Africa. Studies have reported that about 10% of those aged 15-24 have engaged in this exchange in South Africa, 23% in Nigeria and 25% in Uganda. The behaviour has

clusters.headline: China unveils white paper on global governance, calls for stronger Global South representation
clusters.summary: This comes as Beijing seeks to position itself as a champion of multilateralism amid growing geopolitical tensions.

clusters.headline: Singapore regains top spot in competitiveness ranking; Asian economies put up strong showing
clusters.summary: Hong Kong came in second and Taiwan was fourth in IMD’s World Competitiveness Rankings this year.

clusters.headline: The designer behind DR Congo's World Cup suit: 'I wanted to change people's views on Africa'
clusters.summary: Alvin Junior Mak explains the inspiration behind the stylish suits he designed for DR Congo's World Cup team.
